source: Quantum computing focused summaries for APS March Meeting 2026

2026-03-16.overview: Sunday conference opens with the quantum computing track featuring 600 talks across 316 FOCUS, 254 ORAL, 30 INVITED sessions. Featured talks: "Improving single photon emission from vdW materials via atomic engineering" (APS-13528); "Single hole-spin qubit optimization in SOI quantum dots via k⋅p simulations" (APS-2642); "Scalable Diamond Quantum Microchiplets for Integrated Spin-Photon Interfaces" (APS-2643); "Characterizing supercurrent noises using superconducting CPW resonators" (APS-2647); "Optical spin properties of Ce³⁺ centers in thin-film MgAl₂O₄" (APS-14849). This opening day establishes the foundation for the week's quantum hardware research, with emphasis on qubit engineering, quantum dot implementations, and photonic quantum systems. Key research areas include single photon sources for quantum networks, spin qubit optimization in silicon, superconducting resonator characterization, and color center engineering in diamond. Researchers discuss photon emission from 2D materials, hole-spin qubits in silicon-on-insulator structures, integrated spin-photon interfaces using diamond microchiplets, and supercurrent noise characterization. The day highlights advances in quantum information platforms and emerging quantum hardware technologies. Central topics: quantum information, quantum materials, nanophotonics, quantum optics, quantum devices, qubits, noise, quantum light-matter interactions, quantum computing, quantum technology.

2026-03-17.overview: Monday expands the discussion to 701 quantum computing and quantum information talks (310 ORAL, 27 INVITED, 23 POSTER, 337 FOCUS, 4 PANEL sessions). Highlighted presentations: "Probing Topological Phase Transitions in Monolayer Jacutingaite through Magneto-Optics" (APS-12651); "A Universal Foundation Model for Electronic Structure Prediction" (APS-12096); "A study on the effects of noise on ePIC tracking" (APS-12508); "Superconducting magnet design and analysis" (APS-12511); "Second-order Statistical Image Texture Features from Spectral X-ray Images when using Photon-Counting Spectral Detectors" (APS-8848). The day spans topological quantum systems, machine learning for quantum applications, and quantum state characterization. Key sessions address topological phase transition spectroscopy, universal foundation models for predicting quantum system properties, quantum sensing and metrology, quantum error correction pathways, and quantum enhanced measurement. Researchers present advances in quantum simulation, quantum algorithm development, and quantum control techniques. Discussion includes practical quantum computing challenges such as noise characterization, decoherence mechanisms, and fidelity improvements. Advanced topics in quantum information theory complement hands-on experimental quantum physics. Prime research focuses: photonics, quantum phase transitions, quantum materials, quantum physics, quantum information, quantum computing, noise, quantum technology, quantum control, quantum devices.

2026-03-18.overview: Tuesday delivers 635 quantum-focused talks (301 ORAL, 36 INVITED, 279 FOCUS, 18 POSTER, 1 PANEL) bridging quantum physics with broader scientific applications. Notable presentations: "Modeling Stochastic Resonance to Restore Circadian Synchronization in Alzheimer's Disease" (APS-1472); "Bright and Stable Carbon Quantum Dots for Optical and Antimicrobial Applications" (APS-1473); "Autocalibration in the Quantitative Three-Image FRET method enables simplified and quantitative live-cell biosensor imaging" (APS-1474); "Current fluctuations reveal transport mechanisms in a colloidal micropore" (APS-6603); "Chemistry Meets Plasmon Polaritons and Cavity Photons" (APS-6604). Sessions explore quantum sensing applications, quantum metrology techniques, and quantum technology implementation. The track covers quantum information processing, quantum state engineering, quantum simulation methodologies, and quantum control theory. Researchers discuss readout fidelity enhancement, error mitigation strategies, and quantum process characterization. Topics include quantum dot physics, superconducting qubit optimization, trapped ion systems, and photonic quantum hardware. Sessions emphasize practical quantum advantage pathways and near-term quantum computing applications. The day connects fundamental quantum physics discoveries with real-world quantum technology implementations. Central research areas: nanophotonics, noise, quantum dynamics, qubits, quantum optics, quantum molecular science, macroscopic quantum systems, quantum light-matter interactions, quantum enhanced sensing, quantum metrology.

2026-03-19.overview: Wednesday showcases 715 advanced quantum physics and quantum information presentations (238 FOCUS, 37 INVITED, 92 POSTER, 348 ORAL sessions). Premier talks: "From Superfluorescence to Ultrastrong Light-Matter Coupling: Dicke Physics in Solid-State Quantum Matter" (APS-7159); "Role of Spatiotemporal Noise in the Growth and Stability of Ecosystems" (APS-7160); "Kavli Invited Talk: The Road to Quantum Advantage" (APS-7168); "Kavli Invited Talk: Transforming Computing with Atomic Precision" (APS-7169); "Kavli Invited Talk: Translating scientific discoveries to society through industrial partnerships" (APS-7172). This mid-conference day emphasizes quantum advantage achievements, quantum computing scalability, and fundamental quantum phenomena. Sessions cover super-radiance and superfluorescence in quantum systems, light-matter interaction in quantum devices, quantum control of many-body systems, and quantum simulation with engineered platforms. Researchers present Kavli Invited Talks on quantum computing breakthroughs and atomic-precision quantum device manufacturing. Key topics include coherent control of qubits, quantum error correction implementations, quantum algorithm optimization, and quantum gate fidelity measurements. Active discussion on scaling quantum processors and achieving quantum advantage milestones. Critical focus areas: quantum light-matter interactions, quantum phase transitions, nonequilibrium quantum physics, quantum materials, quantum information, quantum computing, quantum technology, quantum devices, quantum networks, quantum sensing.

2026-03-20.overview: Thursday concludes with 290 quantum computing and quantum physics talks (126 FOCUS, 23 INVITED, 141 ORAL) covering emerging quantum technologies and fundamental quantum phenomena. Featured presentations: "Superfluorescence in dense solid-state systems" (APS-13093); "Superradiance in multi-level atoms" (APS-13094); "Directional Super- and Sub-Fluorescence in Single Upconverting Nanocrystals at Room Temperature" (APS-9820); "Superradiance in the breath of a virus" (APS-9821); "Spin Chirality-Induced Spin-electric Coupling and Level Splitting in Three-Center Molecular Magnets" (APS-13139). Final sessions address advanced quantum optical systems, quantum sensing with engineered quantum states, and novel qubit implementations. Researchers discuss quantum technology applications, spin-based quantum systems, and quantum light generation. Sessions cover superradiance in quantum many-body systems, directional quantum light emission, and quantum state engineering in diverse platforms. The closing day synthesizes quantum computing developments and highlights future quantum technology directions. Topics include quantum information processing architectures, quantum sensing sensitivity limits, quantum networking components, and quantum simulation capabilities. Key research directions: macroscopic quantum systems, photonics, quantum light-matter interactions, quantum optics, quantum networks, nanophotonics, qubits, quantum information, quantum molecular science, quantum field theory.
